reviews.review_description: I held every record in this game for a long time. Some of them took months of practice to achieve. After the latest update, however, the game has become so easy that I was able to beat my old record by more than 2.5x with almost no effort.  At around 60,000 points I stopped playing and let Cruise Controls do the work. The score kept increasing without any input from me. In theory, with enough time and battery life, I don't think I could ever lose anymore. The problem is that leaderboards are no longer about skill. They're mostly about patience and endurance. I can still reclaim the last remaining record I lost, but if things stay this way, I probably won't keep playing. If you want leaderboards to reflect player skill, consider adding timed challenges (100, 200, 500, 1,000 points, etc.) where speed matters. Right now, beating a record often means playing for hours, and that's not a very fun competition. But the mere idea of having to play for hours just to beat a record will be a deterrent for many players—especially if I decide to raise the records on all the other levels as well, which I haven't even touched yet since the update.

developer_response.body:
Hi Hansatan, we really appreciate the feedback and your persistence with the game.
We'd love to hear more about the timed challenges idea. If you have some time please contact us - developer.gimbalstudio@gmail.com. 
Some initial ideas,
Limit BUBBLE-T & CRUISE CONTROL to 5 maximum
BUBBLE-T at every 100 score (not 50)
Don't collect score for CRUISE CONTROL saved vehicles
NITRO - Mode (like turbo but faster & more points)

It's fun and addictive to play, it's perfectly set up for this genre.
I used to have a game on my phone where you directed trains and tried not to let them crash and this game has done perfectly in vr.

There's so much variation as well for vehicles and maps, you just start playing and don't even realise where the time has gone.

reviews.review_description: Firstly, this game is very polished! It's well made traffic control type game, you can just sit on the couch and play this fully immersed or in mixed reality. There's 8 different maps that all play very differently and high scores unlockables etc. But here's the thing... this is an Aussie studio right? Where's the utes!? Then Christmas theme is great, there's sleights and bells.... but that's not christmas in Australia! You could have added some aussie humour, had a map where you have to avoid kangaroos, a special outback map or something! Instead it's cosy but kinda generic. Still, the gameplay is addictive as, so I'm giving it 5 stars, and there's no micro transactions the whole thing is playable which I respect. Small issue in passthrough mode the shadows under the cars are somehow....see through? Like I can see the real world in the shadows when I look up close

developer_response.body:
Thanks DavoDivide

We definitely have some more Aussie themed levels in the pipeline for 2026
Will look into the car shadows as well

reviews.review_description: Not for the weak

reviews.review_description:
I was fortunate to have won a copy of this game from Meta Quest Promotions, Giveaways and Referrals on Facebook.
I was just about to go to bed and thought I would give it a try - two hours later and I was still playing it. This game is soooo much fun. Absolutely love it!

reviews.review_title: Great fun to pass the time!
reviews.review_description: The premise of this game is simple, cars are coming from multiple directions and your goal is to make sure they don’t crash into each other. You really only have 2 actions: have the cars speed up or have them slow down. It’s such a simple concept yet as the roads start to fill with cars things get more and more chaotic. If any car crashes into  another, the game ends. Super simple concept, addictively challenging to master. I crashed a lot but I can honestly say I goofed in one way or another (typically because I didn’t see a car coming because I was focused on a different area). If you want something simple to pass the time, I highly recommend this game, especially given the low low price! Get it, you’ll love it!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the glowing review Gentlemansnine
Much appreciated

reviews.review_description:
This is one of those games that is such a simple concept that you don't expect it to be the amazing addictive fun game it is! Let alone have you coming back over and over to try and beat scores or unlock the next levels and vehicles. It just keeps me saying: "One more go." 
Stupid Cars is very challenging for such a simple game but has you coming back for more, making it a very enjoyable challenge. It has lovely music and graphics, is easy to play while seated, and has a mixed-reality mode to enhance the experience. Brilliant fun!

developer_response.body:

We've got a new level coming out soon as well to keep you on your toes

The game play has you on your toes as you have to be quick thinking and decent reactions

reviews.review_title: NOT STUPID, IT’S PRETTY GOOD FUN
reviews.review_description:
A unique take on a great top down game.
Stupid Cars is so much fun, control the traffic with a point and click method works so well, you need eyes everywhere as chaos can take over at any point.

Speeding up or slowing down is all part of the fun, still stuck on the first level currently, but so much reap-lability, colours are vibrant and traffic noises are on point, I can see myself just jumping in at a moments notice, it’s easy to pick up and play.
Very well done to the devs.

developer_response.body:
Thanks GizmoDemi
Really appreciate the kind words

reviews.review_description:
Who knew chaos would be so fun? Reminds of traffic on a Monday but at least you can try to avoid the accidents. I say try... takes time but you will eventually master it.

Plus, with scores, you can try to beat the number of vehicles from the last time you played.
I bet you won't be able to stop playing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review Vexen89

reviews.review_description:
Plays like  an older Shigeru Miyamoto developed game that he mightve dreamed up after witnessing traffic congestion in Tokyo.
Simple gameplay honed to perfection.
Simple level design..but done so well.

Seems easy..all you have to do is make cars go faster or slower to avoid other cars and in other levels make them avoid runaway buses, trains that share the same intersections and cars and motorbikes that make funny loop turns.

I like how it plays on our nature in games, to rush thru things and to take risks to accumulate points quicker. But then there is that fine balance between risk and safety. Reminds me of games like Crossy Road and Ding Dong..where when you die it is always your fault cos you were greedy for speed and points..and then you have to have one more try. One more turn to beat your high score that you've been stuck on for a while. Its the sort of game you will probably see in your sleep.

Congrats devs..shows games don't need a big budget to be awesome fun and that gameplay will always rule.
Thanks to Meta for having it on their new release page..otherwise l would probably have missed this little gem of a game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review vrmorpheus.

reviews.review_description: What a great game, super simple, super great, super fun and super addictive.  This can be updated with Planes, Trains, Trucks and so on,  so much more scope in this playable game.  Well done devs.
